name: Fender CD-60S Solid Top Acoustic Guitar
tag: Best Beginner Acoustic Guitar with Solid Top
introText: The Fender CD-60S is the first acoustic guitar that offers a solid spruce top — which resonates and improves with age — at the entry-level beginner price point, making it the most recommended starter guitar from Fender.
whatWorks: Solid spruce top at the entry price is the defining feature — the CD-60S's solid (not laminate) Sitka spruce top resonates more freely and improves in tone over years of playing, unlike laminate tops that remain static. This is typically a feature of guitars costing $400-600 and is unusual for a $329 instrument.

Easy-to-play neck profile specifically designed for beginners — Fender designed the CD-60S neck profile to be thinner and more comfortable than traditional dreadnought necks, reducing the hand and finger fatigue that causes many beginners to quit. Action is set reasonably low from the factory.
Dreadnought body produces full, projecting sound — the CD-60S's large body produces volume and warmth suited to strumming and flatpicking styles. It projects well in a room without amplification.
Fender brand builds long-term value — selling a Fender when upgrading is easier than selling an unknown brand. For buyers who may resell within 2-3 years, the brand recognition retains more value.
worthKnowing: Laminate back and sides — only the top is solid. The back and sides are laminate mahogany, which is durable and humidity-stable but resonates less freely than solid wood. This is the standard trade-off at this price point.

Factory setup quality varies — like many factory-set guitars, the action may be slightly high from the factory. A professional setup ($40-60) makes a meaningful difference in playability for beginner players whose fingers haven't developed the calluses to overcome high action.

Die-cast tuners are adequate but not premium — the included chrome die-cast tuners hold tune acceptably but are the most common upgrade players make when stepping up to better tuners for extended pitch stability.

No electronics or preamp — the CD-60S is acoustic-only. To amplify for open mics or small performances, you'd need an external clip-on pickup or mic. An electro-acoustic version (CD-60SCE) is available at a higher price.

commonComplaints.title: Factory setup often needs professional attention…
commonComplaints.body: Factory setup often needs professional attention — action and intonation may not be optimized from the factory. A professional setup makes significant difference in comfort for beginner players.

commonComplaints.title: Laminate back and sides limit resonance…
commonComplaints.body: Laminate back and sides limit resonance compared to all-solid guitars — only the top is solid wood. Players upgrading from this guitar to an all-solid model notice the tone difference.

commonComplaints.title: No built-in pickup for amplification…
commonComplaints.body: No built-in pickup for amplification — the CD-60S has no electronics. Open mic, small venue, or recording use requires a clip-on pickup or microphone.

verdict: The Fender CD-60S is the best beginner acoustic guitar under $350. The solid spruce top is a genuine differentiator from laminate competitors, the Fender name provides resale value, and the easy-play neck profile addresses the number one reason beginners quit. Get a professional setup after purchase and you'll have a guitar that grows with your playing for 2-4 years before you feel the need to upgrade.

realBuyers.quote: 18 months in and I can hear the solid top opening up compared to when I got it. It actually sounds better now. Worth the extra $50 over laminate guitars.
The neck profile is genuinely easier to play than the acoustic my friend lent me when I started. My fingers don't ache after a practice session.
Had a luthier setup done ($45) and it plays like a $600 guitar. The bones of this instrument are solid.

metaDescription: Fender CD-60S review for Canada: solid Sitka spruce top, mahogany neck, easy-play profile, dreadnought. ~$329 CAD on Amazon.ca. Best beginner acoustic guitar.
